reason: In classic Excel menus AutoFilter lives at Data > Filter > AutoFilter.
Ctrl+P is the universal Print shortcut; Ctrl+V pastes.
Format Cells > Alignment > Wrap text wraps long text onto multiple lines within the cell.
Excel sheets can be embedded/linked in Word via Insert Object or paste-special.
In classic Word menus Bullets and Numbering is on the Format menu (option labelled C).
Ctrl+B applies bold; Ctrl+U underlines.
Table columns can be deleted (Table > Delete > Columns).
Print Preview displays how the whole file/document will look when printed; it does not print, and it is not limited to tables.
The Office Assistant (Clippy) surfaced help topics and tips; it did not automate commands or autosave.
WordArt inserts decorative/stylised text effects.
Subscript lowers text below the baseline; superscript raises it.
PowerPoint supports Insert > Movies and Sounds on a slide.
Pictures can be inserted onto slides.
Format > Background changes slide background colour/fill.
PowerPoint provides an organization chart / SmartArt hierarchy diagram.
The Relationships window joins tables (and queries can be added to it); reports and forms have no relationship definitions.
Access forms are the standard UI for viewing and entering record data.
Forms are bound to tables or queries, never to reports.
Queries retrieve/filter data from tables (or other queries); forms and reports are consumers, not sources to query.
Reports can be built/edited in Design View (also via wizard, but Design View is the authoring surface), so Yes.
